title: Step 3: If  is enabled, enter the verification code.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To create a project for an organization, you must be either an
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) or an
[Organization Project Creator](<Organization Project Creator>).

When you create a project, you are added as an
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) for the project.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
Do one of the following steps:

- Click New Project.

- Expand the Projects menu in the navigation
  bar, then click + New Project.

* Enter the name for your new project.

  > **Important:** The project name has the following limits:
  >
  > - Can't exceed 64 characters.
  >
  > - Restricted to letters, numbers, spaces, dashes, and
  >   underscores.
  >
  > Don't include [sensitive information](sensitive-info) in
  > your project name.

* Click Next.

- For existing Atlas users, enter their username. Usually, this
  is the email the person used to register.

- For new Atlas users, enter their email address to send an
  invitation.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To view a project, you must either:

- Be an [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>)

- Receive an [invitation](atlas_add_users) that grants access to
  the project. An [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) or [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) can invite users to projects.

> **Important:** 
>
> Project Invitation Deprecation
>
> The Atlas release on September 13, 2023 deprecates project
> invitations.
>
> When you invite an organization member to projects within the
> organization, the user is automatically granted access to
> those projects and doesn't receive any invitations. When you invite a
> user to projects in an organization that the user doesn't belong to,
> the user receives a single invitation to the organization, which
> includes access to all of the projects that you grant them access to.
> Invitations expire after 30 days.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
A Backup Compliance Policy icon appears next to each project name that has a
[Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy).

sections.heading: Update a Project
sections.body: You can update project details such as the project name and tags.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body: To update a project, you must have [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.

sections.heading: Step 1: Update project details.
sections.body: You can update the project name and tags in the **Project Settings** page.

sections.heading: Move a Project
sections.body:
When you move a project to another Atlas organization, Atlas
copies the project users and their respective roles to the
same project in the destination organization. However, Atlas
doesn't carry over teams assigned to the project because you define
teams at the organization level.

When you move projects across organizations, your changes take effect
immediately. The move doesn't:

- Impact cluster uptime or current cluster configuration.

- Cause downtime for your cluster or changes to your
  connection string.

Atlas carries over VPC peering connections and private endpoint
configurations to the destination organization. You don't need to
recreate them.

> **Important:** Atlas removes existing API Keys after moving the project. You
> must [create a new API Key](create-project-api-key)
> after moving the project.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
To move a project to another Atlas organization, you must be an
[Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) for both the current and the destination
organization.

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
To move a project for an organization:

For the project you want to move:

- Click .

- Click Move Project.

The Move Project dialog box appears.

> **Note:** 
>
> Billing a Moved Project
>
> An [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) of two different Organizations
> can move projects between those Organizations at any time. Usage in
> any particular project during any particular hour is accrued to the
> Organization under which the project was at that time.
>
> For example, an [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) owns the **Telecomm** and
> **Storage** Organizations in Atlas. They decide to move
> their **Backup** project from **Telecomm** to **Storage** at
> 11:40 am.
>
> The **Telecomm** project is billed for the full 11:00 to 11:59
> am hour. **Storage** starts getting billed at 12:00 pm.

sections.heading: Delete a Project
sections.body:
> **Note:** If you have a [Backup Compliance Policy enabled](backup-compliance-policy), you
> can't delete the project if any snapshots exist.

sections.heading: Prerequisites
sections.body:
- To delete a project for an organization, you must either have the
  [Project Owner](<Project Owner>) role for the project or have the
  [Organization Owner](<Organization Owner>) role for the project's organization.

- You must terminate any Atlas Services apps and/or MongoDB Charts instances.

- The project has no outstanding invoices.

- The project has no active clusters.
  [Terminate active clusters](terminate-cluster) or
  [Flex clusters](terminate-flex-cluster) in the project
  before you delete it.

- The project has no configured [private endpoint connections](private-endpoint).

- The project has no active [federated database instances](atlas-data-federation).

sections.heading: Procedure
sections.body:
You can delete from the organization's **Projects** view
or the project's **Project Setting** view.

To delete a project from the organization's
**Projects** view:

After verifying, click Delete Project again.

To delete from the project's **Project Setting** view:

After verifying, click Delete Project again.

sections.heading: Manage Projects with the Atlas CLI
sections.body: You can use the Atlas CLI to manage projects programmatically.

sections.heading: Create a Project
sections.body:
To create a new project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects create <projectName> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-create).

sections.heading: View Projects
sections.body:
To list all projects using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas projects list [options]

```To return the details for the project you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:```sh

atlas projects describe <ID> [options]

```To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas projects list](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-list) and
[atlas projects describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-describe).Related Links* [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

* [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

sections.heading: Update a Project
sections.body:
To update a project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects update <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects update](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-update).

sections.heading: Delete a Project
sections.body:
To delete a project using the
Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas projects delete <ID> [options]

```

To learn more about the command syntax and parameters, see the
Atlas CLI documentation for [atlas projects delete](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-projects-delete).

sections.heading: Manage Projects with the Atlas Admin API
sections.body: You can use the Atlas Administration API to manage projects programmatically.

sections.heading: Create a Project
sections.body:
To create a project for an organization using the API, see
[Create One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-creategroup)

sections.heading: View Projects
sections.body:
To view a project using the API, see
[Return One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroup) or
[Return One Project using Its Name](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-getgroupbyname)

sections.heading: Update a Project
sections.body:
To update a project using the API, see
[Update One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-updateproject)

sections.heading: Delete a Project
sections.body:
To delete a project for an organization using the API, see
[Remove One Project](api/doc/atlas-admin-api-v2/operation/operation-deletegroup)
